message:
fix: propagate context and cancel AI goroutines on WS disconnect (#10016)

* 🐛 fix: suppress GA4 auth errors in both global error handlers (#9994)

Add UnauthenticatedError/UnauthorizedError guards to the unhandledrejection
handler AND the window error handler for defense-in-depth. Broaden the
message check to catch both 'No authentication token available' (from
api.ts UnauthenticatedError) and 'No authentication token' (from fetcher
utils) so neither path emits false-positive GA4 auth alerts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>

* 🐛 fix: propagate context and cancel AI goroutines on WebSocket disconnect (#9997)

Three fixes for goroutine leaks and context propagation debt in AI/WebSocket handlers:

1. handleMessage now accepts a context derived from the WebSocket connection
   lifecycle (connCtx). Non-streaming handlers like kubectl are cancelled
   immediately when the client disconnects instead of running until their
   own timeout expires.

2. KubectlProxy.ExecuteWithContext derives the exec deadline from a parent
   context so client disconnect kills the kubectl process immediately.
   The original Execute method is preserved as a thin wrapper for
   backward compatibility.

3. cancelAllChatsForConn explicitly cancels and removes all active chat
   sessions owned by a disconnecting WebSocket connection. Previously,
   entries lingered in activeChatCtxs until the per-chat defer ran,
   which could be delayed if the goroutine was blocked on the semaphore
   or a slow LLM provider.

4. handleChatMessage (non-streaming) accepts an optional parent context
   via variadic parameter so callers can propagate connection lifecycle.
   Existing callers are unaffected (defaults to context.Background).

Signed-off-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>

---------

Signed-off-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>
Co-authored-by: Reviewer Agent <reviewer@claude-dev.local>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
